You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>

considera os findings abaixo, corrige e atualiza o PR:

1. Alta: [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L193](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L193) transforma a aplicação em um proxy autenticado para escrever no GitHub com um PAT de `repo` + `project`, mas sem qualquer throttling, quota ou controle anti-abuso antes dos `fetch` externos. Como o produto mantém auto-cadastro em [src/app/auth/register/page.tsx#L99](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/auth/register/page.tsx#L99), uma conta abusiva consegue automatizar abertura massiva de issues e uploads de anexos no repositório.

2. Média: [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L150](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L150) só trata falha da mutation GraphQL via `response.ok`. No GitHub GraphQL é comum receber `200` com `errors` quando a mutation falha; nesse cenário [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L167](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L167) ainda retorna `true`, então o cliente recebe `projectLinked: true` mesmo sem o card entrar no Project.

3. Média: [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L243](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L243) envia e commita os anexos no repositório antes de [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L271](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L271) criar a issue. Se a criação da issue falhar depois, a API devolve erro mas os arquivos em `bug-attachments/` já ficaram no histórico sem issue correspondente.

Revisei `main...HEAD` do branch atual e rodei `npx vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts tests/integration/api/bug-reports.test.ts`; os 8 testes passam, mas a suíte não cobre esses três cenários.

considera os findings abaixo, corrige e atualiza o PR:

1. Alta: [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L193](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L193) transforma a aplicação em um proxy autenticado para escrever no GitHub com um PAT de `repo` + `project`, mas sem qualquer throttling, quota ou controle anti-abuso antes dos `fetch` externos. Como o produto mantém auto-cadastro em [src/app/auth/register/page.tsx#L99](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/auth/register/page.tsx#L99), uma conta abusiva consegue automatizar abertura massiva de issues e uploads de anexos no repositório.

2. Média: [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L150](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L150) só trata falha da mutation GraphQL via `response.ok`. No GitHub GraphQL é comum receber `200` com `errors` quando a mutation falha; nesse cenário [src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L167](/Users/victor/conductor/workspaces/expense-control/conakry/src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts#L167) ainda retorna `true`, então o cliente recebe `projectLinked: true` mesmo sem o card entrar no Project.

3. Se a criação da issue falhar depois, a API devolve erro mas os arquivos em `bug-attachments/` já ficaram no histórico sem issue correspondente.

Revisei `main...HEAD` do branch atual e rodei `npx vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts tests/integration/api/bug-reports.test.ts`; os 8 testes passam, mas a suíte não cobre esses três cenários.

content.text: Looking at the three findings, let me start by reading the current code.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the three findings:

1. **Rate limiting** - No throttling/quota on the bug report endpoint. Need to add per-user rate limiting.
2. **GraphQL error handling** - `addIssueToProject` only checks `response.ok` but GraphQL can return 200 with `errors`. Need to check for `errors` in the response body.
3. **Upload before issue creation** - Images are uploaded first, then issue is created. If issue creation fails, orphan files remain. Need to reorder: create issue first, then upload images and update the issue body.

Let me fix all three:

1. Add a simple in-memory rate limiter (token bucket or sliding window per user)


2. Check `errors` field in GraphQL responses in `addIssueToProject`
3. Reorder the workflow: create the issue first with just the text body, upload images afterward, then update the issue with image URLs. This way if issue creation fails, nothing gets orphaned, and if uploads fail later, at least the issue exists.

Let me implement these changes.

content.text:
Now I understand all three issues clearly. Let me fix them:

1. **Rate limiting** - Add per-user in-memory rate limiter
2. **GraphQL error handling** - Check `errors` field in response body, not just `response.ok`
3. **Upload ordering** - Create issue first, then upload images, then update the issue body

input.command:
git add src/app/api/bug-reports/route.ts tests/integration/api/bug-reports.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: address bug report API security and reliability findings

1. Add per-user rate limiting (5 reports per 15min window) to prevent
   abuse via automated issue/attachment creation
2. Check GraphQL `errors` field in addIssueToProject responses, not just
   HTTP status — fixes false `projectLinked: true` on mutation failures
3. Reorder flow: create issue first, then upload images and PATCH body —
   prevents orphan attachments when issue creation fails

Adds 4 new integration tests covering rate limiting, GraphQL error
handling, and the no-orphan-uploads guarantee.
EOF
)"

content.text:
Pushed. Resumo das correções:

**Finding 1 (Alta) — Rate limiting:** Adicionei um rate limiter in-memory por userId com sliding window de 15 minutos, máximo 5 reports. Retorna `429` quando excedido. Nenhuma chamada externa é feita se o rate limit for atingido.

**Finding 2 (Média) — GraphQL errors:** `addIssueToProject` agora verifica o campo `errors` no body da resposta em ambas as chamadas GraphQL (fetch project ID e mutation). Retorna `false` ao invés de `true` quando há erros, mesmo com HTTP 200.

**Finding 3 (Média) — Orphan attachments:** Reordenei o fluxo: a issue é criada primeiro (sem imagens), depois as imagens são uploaded, e por fim o body da issue é atualizado via PATCH. Se a criação da issue falhar, nenhum upload acontece.

4 novos testes cobrem exatamente esses cenários. Todos os 12 testes passam.
